The correct answer is **\boxed{A}**.

This subroutine implements the **Euclidean algorithm** to compute the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two positive integers \( I \) and \( J \), storing the result in \( K \). The algorithm repeatedly replaces the larger number with its remainder when divided by the smaller number until one of them becomes zero, at which point the other is the GCD. The logic matches the standard iterative approach for finding the GCD.
